text: Biocatalysis has become a major theme of modern chemical synthesis of organic compounds. Almost all enzymes used in enzyme-assisted organic synthesis (enzyme organic synthesis) work by reacting molecules in their ground state. However, the development of biocatalysis in the last 25 years has drawn increasing attention to enzymes that act through excited states by activating molecules through photoexcitation. Photobiocatalysis involves a variety of mechanisms that are highly complementary. This review aims at summarizing the recent advances in photobiocatalysis for organic synthesis up to December 2022, in addition to reviewing relevant literature and highlighting differences between the various mechanisms involved, which is a primary goal of this review. Rather than being systematic, we shall focus on the interplay between the photochemical activation step and the associated enzymatic catalysis. In addition to highlighting structural elucidation, substrate scope and enzyme engineering strategies, we intend to facilitate the future development of this important area of biocatalysis by distinguishing between different mechanisms of photobiocatalysis.
